```java
public class CPVal {
    public enum Kind { UNDEF, INT_CONST, BOOL_CONST, NAC }
    
    public final Kind kind;
    public final int intVal;
    public final boolean boolVal;
    
    private CPVal(Kind k, int i, boolean b) { kind = k; intVal = i; boolVal = b; }
    
    public static final CPVal UNDEF = new CPVal(Kind.UNDEF, 0, false);
    public static final CPVal NAC   = new CPVal(Kind.NAC,   0, false);
    
    public static CPVal ofInt(int v)    { return new CPVal(Kind.INT_CONST,  v, false); }
    public static CPVal ofBool(boolean v){ return new CPVal(Kind.BOOL_CONST, 0, v);    }
    
    public boolean isConst() { return kind == Kind.INT_CONST || kind == Kind.BOOL_CONST; }
    public boolean isTrue()  { return kind == Kind.BOOL_CONST && boolVal; }
    public boolean isFalse() { return kind == Kind.BOOL_CONST && !boolVal; }
    
    /** Emit as TACoJava2 source literal (null if not a constant) */
    public String toSource() {
        switch (kind) {
            case INT_CONST:
                if (intVal < 0) return "- " + (-intVal); // TACoJava2 allows IntegerLiteralWithNegSign
                return "" + intVal;
            case BOOL_CONST:
                return boolVal ? "true" : "false";
            default:
                return null;
        }
    }
    
    /** Lattice meet operation */
    public static CPVal meet(CPVal a, CPVal b) {
        if (a.kind == Kind.UNDEF) return b;
        if (b.kind == Kind.UNDEF) return a;
        if (a.kind == Kind.NAC   || b.kind == Kind.NAC) return NAC;
        if (a.kind == Kind.INT_CONST && b.kind == Kind.INT_CONST)
            return a.intVal == b.intVal ? a : NAC;
        if (a.kind == Kind.BOOL_CONST && b.kind == Kind.BOOL_CONST)
            return a.boolVal == b.boolVal ? a : NAC;
        return NAC;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String toString() {
        switch (kind) {
            case UNDEF: return "UNDEF";
            case NAC:   return "NAC";
            case INT_CONST:  return "INT(" + intVal + ")";
            case BOOL_CONST: return "BOOL(" + boolVal + ")";
        }
        return "?";
    }
}
```

### SymbolTableVisitor.java

This needs to collect:
- Class hierarchy
- Class fields (name → type)
- Method signatures (params, locals, return type)
- Method AST nodes (for call graph traversal)
- MainClass AST node

```java
package visitor;

import syntaxtree.*;
import java.util.*;

public class SymbolTableVisitor extends GJDepthFirst<String, String> {
    // ============= Class Hierarchy =============
    public final Map<String, String>       parentOf     = new LinkedHashMap<>();
    public final Map<String, Set<String>>  childrenOf   = new LinkedHashMap<>();
    
    // ============= Class Content =============
    // class -> (fieldName -> type)
    public final Map<String, Map<String, String>> classFields = new LinkedHashMap<>();
    // classes in declaration order
    public final List<String> allClasses = new ArrayList<>();
    
    // ============= Method Info =============
    // class -> method -> type
    public final Map<String, Map<String, String>> methodReturnType = new LinkedHashMap<>();
    // class -> method -> list of param names (in order)
    public final Map<String, Map<String, List<String>>> methodParams = new LinkedHashMap<>();
    // class -> method -> (varName -> type) for all locals + params
    public final Map<String, Map<String, Map<String, String>>> methodVars = new LinkedHashMap<>();
    // class -> methods in declaration order
    public final Map<String, List<String>> classMethodOrder = new LinkedHashMap<>();
    
    // ============= AST Nodes =============
    public MainClass mainClassNode = null;
    // "Class.method" -> AST node
    public final Map<String, MethodDeclaration> methodNodes = new LinkedHashMap<>();
    
    // ======= Internal traversal state =======
    private String  currentClass  = null;
    private String  currentMethod = null;
    private boolean inClassField  = false;  // true when inside a class but outside a method
    
    // ===== Public name of main class =====
    public String mainClassName = null;
    
    // ===== Helper: getVarType(class, method, varName) =====
    public String getVarType(String className, String methodName, String varName) {
        // 1. check method locals/params
        Map<String, Map<String, String>> meths = methodVars.get(className);
        if (meths != null) {
            Map<String, String> vars = meths.get(methodName);
            if (vars != null && vars.containsKey(varName)) return vars.get(varName);
        }
        // 2. check class fields (including inherited)
        String cls = className;
        while (cls != null) {
            Map<String, String> fields = classFields.get(cls);
            if (fields != null && fields.containsKey(varName)) return fields.get(varName);
            cls = parentOf.get(cls);
        }
        return null;
    }
    
    // ===== Helper: allSubclasses(className) =====
    public Set<String> allSubclasses(String className) {
        Set<String> result = new HashSet<>();
        Deque<String> queue = new ArrayDeque<>();
        Set<String> direct = childrenOf.get(className);
        if (direct != null) queue.addAll(direct);
        while (!queue.isEmpty()) {
            String c = queue.poll();
            if (result.add(c)) {
                Set<String> ch = childrenOf.get(c);
                if (ch != null) queue.addAll(ch);
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
    
    // ===== Helper: resolveMethod(startClass, methodName) =====
    // Returns the class in the hierarchy that defines the method (following inheritance)
    public String resolveMethod(String startClass, String methodName) {
        String c = startClass;
        while (c != null) {
            Map<String, String> rtypes = methodReturnType.get(c);
            if (rtypes != null && rtypes.containsKey(methodName)) return c;
            c = parentOf.get(c);
        }
        return null;
    }
    
    // ===== Visitor methods =====
    
    @Override
    public String visit(Goal n, String argu) {
        n.f0.accept(this, argu); // MainClass
        n.f1.accept(this, argu); // TypeDeclarations
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(MainClass n, String argu) {
        mainClassNode  = n;
        mainClassName  = n.f1.accept(this, argu);
        currentClass   = mainClassName;
        
        allClasses.add(mainClassName);
        parentOf.put(mainClassName, null);
        childrenOf.put(mainClassName, new HashSet<>());
        classFields.put(mainClassName, new LinkedHashMap<>());
        classMethodOrder.put(mainClassName, new ArrayList<>());
        
        // main is a special method
        initMethod(mainClassName, "main", "void");
        currentMethod = "main";
        // parameter: String[] args — treat as NAC, not in symbol table
        
        // local var declarations
        n.f14.accept(this, argu);
        
        currentMethod = null;
        currentClass  = null;
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(TypeDeclaration n, String argu) {
        n.f0.accept(this, argu);
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(ClassDeclaration n, String argu) {
        currentClass = n.f1.accept(this, argu);
        allClasses.add(currentClass);
        parentOf.put(currentClass, null);
        childrenOf.put(currentClass, new HashSet<>());
        classFields.put(currentClass, new LinkedHashMap<>());
        classMethodOrder.put(currentClass, new ArrayList<>());
        
        inClassField = true;
        n.f3.accept(this, argu); // VarDeclarations (fields)
        inClassField = false;
        n.f4.accept(this, argu); // MethodDeclarations
        
        currentClass = null;
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(ClassExtendsDeclaration n, String argu) {
        currentClass   = n.f1.accept(this, argu);
        String parent  = n.f3.accept(this, argu);
        allClasses.add(currentClass);
        parentOf.put(currentClass, parent);
        childrenOf.computeIfAbsent(parent, k -> new HashSet<>()).add(currentClass);
        childrenOf.put(currentClass, new HashSet<>());
        classFields.put(currentClass, new LinkedHashMap<>());
        classMethodOrder.put(currentClass, new ArrayList<>());
        
        inClassField = true;
        n.f5.accept(this, argu); // VarDeclarations (fields)
        inClassField = false;
        n.f6.accept(this, argu); // MethodDeclarations
        
        currentClass = null;
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(VarDeclaration n, String argu) {
        String type = typeToString(n.f0);
        String name = n.f1.accept(this, argu);
        
        if (inClassField) {
            classFields.get(currentClass).put(name, type);
        } else {
            // local variable in a method
            methodVars.get(currentClass).get(currentMethod).put(name, type);
        }
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(MethodDeclaration n, String argu) {
        String returnType  = typeToString(n.f1);
        currentMethod      = n.f2.accept(this, argu);
        
        classMethodOrder.computeIfAbsent(currentClass, k -> new ArrayList<>()).add(currentMethod);
        initMethod(currentClass, currentMethod, returnType);
        
        // Store AST node for call graph traversal
        methodNodes.put(currentClass + "." + currentMethod, n);
        
        // Parameters
        n.f4.accept(this, argu); // FormalParameterList?
        
        // Local var declarations
        n.f7.accept(this, argu); // VarDeclarations
        
        currentMethod = null;
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(FormalParameterList n, String argu) {
        n.f0.accept(this, argu);
        n.f1.accept(this, argu);
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(FormalParameter n, String argu) {
        String type = typeToString(n.f0);
        String name = n.f1.accept(this, argu);
        methodParams.get(currentClass).get(currentMethod).add(name);
        methodVars.get(currentClass).get(currentMethod).put(name, type);
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(FormalParameterRest n, String argu) {
        return n.f1.accept(this, argu);
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(Identifier n, String argu) {
        return n.f0.tokenImage;
    }
    
    // ========= Private Helpers =========
    
    private void initMethod(String cls, String meth, String retType) {
        methodReturnType.computeIfAbsent(cls, k -> new LinkedHashMap<>()).put(meth, retType);
        methodParams    .computeIfAbsent(cls, k -> new LinkedHashMap<>()).put(meth, new ArrayList<>());
        methodVars      .computeIfAbsent(cls, k -> new LinkedHashMap<>()).put(meth, new LinkedHashMap<>());
    }
    
    public String typeToString(Type t) {
        Node choice = t.f0.choice;
        if (choice instanceof ArrayType)    return "int[]";
        if (choice instanceof BooleanType)  return "boolean";
        if (choice instanceof IntegerType)  return "int";
        if (choice instanceof Identifier)   return ((Identifier) choice).f0.tokenImage;
        return "unknown";
    }
    
    public String typeToSource(Type t) {
        Node choice = t.f0.choice;
        if (choice instanceof ArrayType)    return "int []";
        if (choice instanceof BooleanType)  return "boolean";
        if (choice instanceof IntegerType)  return "int";
        if (choice instanceof Identifier)   return ((Identifier) choice).f0.tokenImage;
        return "unknown";
    }
}
```

### CallGraphVisitor.java

This builds the call graph using CHA and also identifies pure (side-effect-free) methods.

```java
package visitor;

import syntaxtree.*;
import java.util.*;

public class CallGraphVisitor extends GJDepthFirst<String, String> {
    private final SymbolTableVisitor st;
    
    public final Set<String> reachable   = new LinkedHashSet<>();
    private final Deque<String> worklist = new ArrayDeque<>();
    
    private String currentClass;
    private String currentMethod;
    
    // Side-effect analysis: methods that have no field writes and call only pure methods
    public final Set<String> pureMethods = new HashSet<>();
    
    public CallGraphVisitor(SymbolTableVisitor st) {
        this.st = st;
    }
    
    /** Entry point: seed with main and process the worklist */
    public void build() {
        String mainEntry = st.mainClassName + ".main";
        reachable.add(mainEntry);
        worklist.add(mainEntry);
        
        while (!worklist.isEmpty()) {
            String methId = worklist.poll();
            int dot = methId.lastIndexOf('.');
            currentClass  = methId.substring(0, dot);
            currentMethod = methId.substring(dot + 1);
            
            if (currentMethod.equals("main")) {
                st.mainClassNode.accept(this, null);
            } else {
                MethodDeclaration md = st.methodNodes.get(methId);
                if (md != null) md.accept(this, null);
            }
        }
        
        computePureMethods();
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(MessageSend n, String argu) {
        // Determine type of receiver (PrimaryExpression)
        String receiverType = getPrimaryType(n.f0);
        String calledMethod  = n.f2.f0.tokenImage;
        
        if (receiverType != null) {
            // CHA: include method in receiverType and all subclasses
            Set<String> candidates = new HashSet<>();
            candidates.add(receiverType);
            candidates.addAll(st.allSubclasses(receiverType));
            
            for (String cls : candidates) {
                String defClass = st.resolveMethod(cls, calledMethod);
                if (defClass != null) {
                    String calleeId = defClass + "." + calledMethod;
                    if (reachable.add(calleeId)) {
                        worklist.add(calleeId);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Also visit arguments in case they contain message sends (not in TACoJava, but to be safe)
        n.f4.accept(this, argu);
        return null;
    }
    
    // Helper: get declared type of a PrimaryExpression in current context
    private String getPrimaryType(PrimaryExpression pe) {
        Node choice = pe.f0.choice;
        if (choice instanceof Identifier) {
            String varName = ((Identifier) choice).f0.tokenImage;
            return st.getVarType(currentClass, currentMethod, varName);
        }
        if (choice instanceof ThisExpression) {
            return currentClass;
        }
        if (choice instanceof AllocationExpression) {
            return ((AllocationExpression) choice).f1.f0.tokenImage;
        }
        return null;
    }
    
    /** Compute pure methods: no field assignments, only calls to pure methods */
    private void computePureMethods() {
        // Iterative: start with methods that have no FieldAssignmentStatement
        // Then propagate: a method is pure if all its callees are pure
        
        // Build a "may have side effects" set
        Set<String> mayHaveSideEffects = new HashSet<>();
        
        // Phase 1: mark all reachable methods with field assignments
        for (String methId : reachable) {
            if (methId.endsWith(".main")) {
                mayHaveSideEffects.add(methId); // main is not pure (has print)
                continue;
            }
            MethodDeclaration md = st.methodNodes.get(methId);
            if (md != null) {
                SideEffectChecker sec = new SideEffectChecker();
                md.accept(sec, null);
                if (sec.hasDirectSideEffect) {
                    mayHaveSideEffects.add(methId);
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Phase 2: propagate side effects through call graph
        // Build a calls-graph (which reachable methods each method calls)
        // For simplicity: iterate until fixpoint
        // If a method calls a method that may have side effects → it also may have side effects
        boolean changed = true;
        while (changed) {
            changed = false;
            for (String methId : reachable) {
                if (mayHaveSideEffects.contains(methId)) continue;
                MethodDeclaration md = st.methodNodes.get(methId);
                if (md == null) continue;
                
                CalleeCollector cc = new CalleeCollector(st, methId);
                md.accept(cc, null);
                for (String callee : cc.callees) {
                    if (mayHaveSideEffects.contains(callee)) {
                        mayHaveSideEffects.add(methId);
                        changed = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
        // All reachable methods NOT in mayHaveSideEffects are pure
        for (String methId : reachable) {
            if (!mayHaveSideEffects.contains(methId)) {
                pureMethods.add(methId);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // ========= Inner helper visitors =========
    
    /** Checks if a method body directly has field assignments or print statements */
    static class SideEffectChecker extends GJDepthFirst<Object, Object> {
        boolean hasDirectSideEffect = false;
        
        @Override
        public Object visit(FieldAssignmentStatement n, Object argu) {
            hasDirectSideEffect = true;
            return null;
        }
        
        @Override
        public Object visit(PrintStatement n, Object argu) {
            hasDirectSideEffect = true;
            return null;
        }
    }
    
    /** Collects direct callees of a method */
    static class CalleeCollector extends GJDepthFirst<Object, Object> {
        final SymbolTableVisitor st;
        final String methodId;
        final String currentClass;
        final String currentMethod;
        final Set<String> callees = new HashSet<>();
        
        CalleeCollector(SymbolTableVisitor st, String methId) {
            this.st = st;
            this.methodId = methId;
            int dot = methId.lastIndexOf('.');
            this.currentClass  = methId.substring(0, dot);
            this.currentMethod = methId.substring(dot + 1);
        }
        
        @Override
        public Object visit(MessageSend n, Object argu) {
            String receiverType = getPrimaryType(n.f0);
            String calledMethod  = n.f2.f0.tokenImage;
            if (receiverType != null) {
                Set<String> candidates = new HashSet<>();
                candidates.add(receiverType);
                candidates.addAll(st.allSubclasses(receiverType));
                for (String cls : candidates) {
                    String defClass = st.resolveMethod(cls, calledMethod);
                    if (defClass != null) {
                        callees.add(defClass + "." + calledMethod);
                    }
                }
            }
            n.f4.accept(this, argu);
            return null;
        }
        
        private String getPrimaryType(PrimaryExpression pe) {
            Node choice = pe.f0.choice;
            if (choice instanceof Identifier) {
                return st.getVarType(currentClass, currentMethod, ((Identifier)choice).f0.tokenImage);
            }
            if (choice instanceof ThisExpression) return currentClass;
            if (choice instanceof AllocationExpression)
                return ((AllocationExpression)choice).f1.f0.tokenImage;
            return null;
        }
    }
}
```

Now for the CodeGenVisitor. This is the main complex piece. Let me think about the structure:

The CodeGenVisitor needs to:
1. Traverse the AST in program order
2. Maintain a CP state per method
3. Emit TACoJava2 code with constants substituted

The key methods:

```java
visit(Goal n): emit MainClass + TypeDeclarations
visit(MainClass n): emit class, init CP state, emit statements
visit(ClassDeclaration n): emit class (skip if unused? - no, classes might have reachable methods)
visit(MethodDeclaration n): skip if not reachable; else init CP state, emit method
visit(AssignmentStatement n): eval RHS, update state, emit or skip
visit(IfStatement n): check condition, emit surviving branch(es)
visit(WhileStatement n): check condition, conservatively handle
visit(PrintStatement n): emit with substituted constant
visit(ForStatement n): handle conservatively
```

For expression handling, I need methods that return the string representation with constant substitution:
- evalExpr(Expression e) → CPVal (what value does this expression have?)
- emitExpr(Expression e) → String (what to emit in the output?)

Wait, in TACoJava2, the `Expression` nonterminal is the same as in TACoJava, but `ConstOrId` can appear where `Identifier` appeared before. So the expression structure is the same - I emit the same structure but with ConstOrId substitutions.

For `PlusExpression`: `x + y` → if x=5, y=3 → output `5 + 3`? 

Wait, but the RHS of an AssignmentStatement must be an `Expression()`. And in TACoJava2's grammar, `PlusExpression` is `ConstOrId() "+" ConstOrId()`. So yes, I can output `5 + 3` as a PlusExpression in TACoJava2.

And the result of `5 + 3` would be `CONST(8)`, but do I output the simplified result or the expression? The CP analysis might compute x = 5 + 3 = 8, so the assignment `z = x + y` → if x=5 and y=3, then z=8. But in the output, do I write `z = 8;` or `z = 5 + 3;`?

Actually, I should write `z = 5 + 3;` because that's the TACoJava2 expression (with ConstOrId). The variable z will then be substituted by 8 at all its use points (since z's CP value is 8). The dead assignment elimination will then remove `z = 5 + 3;` if z is dead.

Wait but then is `z = 5 + 3;` valid TACoJava2? Yes! TACoJava2 has `ConstOrId() "+" ConstOrId()` in PlusExpression. So `5 + 3` is valid. And `z = 5 + 3;` with z declared as `int z;` in the VarDeclaration is valid.

So the strategy is:
1. Emit the expression with constants substituted at each operand position
2. The assignment's LHS still gets the computed CP value
3. Dead assignments get elided

But what about `z = 8`? That would be an AssignmentStatement with `z = PrimaryExpression` where PrimaryExpression is `IntegerLiteral(8)`. Is that valid TACoJava2? Yes! Looking at the grammar:
```
AssignmentStatement: Identifier() "=" Expression() ";"
Expression: ... | PrimaryExpression()
PrimaryExpression: IntegerLiteral() | ...
```

So `z = 8;` is a valid AssignmentStatement.

Actually, if x=5 and y=3, I should emit `z = 5 + 3;` (substituting x→5 and y→3 in the operands). The assignment itself remains, and z gets CP value 8. If z is later dead, the assignment is removed.

Alternatively, I could emit `z = 8;` directly (folding the constant). This is simpler and achieves the same result. Let me do constant folding:

If eval(expr) returns CONST(v), emit `z = v;` (the folded constant as a PrimaryExpression).
If eval(expr) returns NAC, emit `z = emit_expr(expr);` with constants substituted in operands.

This way:
- `z = x + y` where x=5, y=3 → `z = 8;` (folded)
- `z = x + y` where x=5, y=NAC → `z = 5 + y;` (partial substitution)
- `z = x + y` where x=NAC, y=NAC → `z = x + y;` (no substitution)

This is cleaner. Let me implement it this way.

Actually wait - can we directly emit `z = 8;`? `8` is a PlainIntegerLiteral, and `z = 8` is AssignmentStatement with Expression = PrimaryExpression = IntegerLiteral. Yes, that's valid.

But what about negative constants? If we fold `z = x - y` where x=2, y=5 → z = -3. We need to emit `z = - 3;` (TACoJava2 has IntegerLiteralWithNegSign). Is that a valid Expression? Looking at TACoJava2:
```
Expression: ... | PrimaryExpression()
PrimaryExpression: IntegerLiteral() | ...
IntegerLiteral: IntegerLiteralWithNegSign() | ...
IntegerLiteralWithNegSign: "-" INTEGER_LITERAL
```

So `z = -3;` would be `z = IntegerLiteralWithNegSign;` which is valid. 

OK so my plan: when emitting a RHS expression, compute its CP value first. If it's a constant, emit the constant directly. If not, emit the expression with constants substituted in operand positions.

Let me now write the CodeGenVisitor. I'll use a `PrintWriter` instead of StringBuilder for cleaner output.

Actually, let me use StringBuilder and emit to System.out at the end.

Here is the CodeGenVisitor. It's going to be long but I'll write it completely:

```java
package visitor;

import syntaxtree.*;
import java.util.*;

public class CodeGenVisitor extends GJDepthFirst<String, String> {
    private final SymbolTableVisitor st;
    private final Set<String> reachable;
    private final Set<String> pureMethods;
    
    private final StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
    private int indent = 0;
    
    // Current scope
    private String currentClass;
    private String currentMethod;
    
    // CP state for current method
    private Map<String, CPVal> cpState = new HashMap<>();
    
    // Variables used in non-ConstOrId positions (can't substitute)
    // These must be computed per method before the CP pass
    private Set<String> nonSubstitutableVars = new HashSet<>();
    
    public CodeGenVisitor(SymbolTableVisitor st, Set<String> reachable, Set<String> pureMethods) {
        this.st = st;
        this.reachable = reachable;
        this.pureMethods = pureMethods;
    }
    
    public String getOutput() { return sb.toString(); }
    
    // ======= Output helpers =======
    
    private void emit(String s) { sb.append(s); }
    private void emitln(String s) { sb.append(s).append('\n'); }
    private void nl() { sb.append('\n'); emit(ind()); }
    private String ind() { 
        StringBuilder s = new StringBuilder();
        for (int i = 0; i < indent; i++) s.append("   ");
        return s.toString();
    }
    
    // ======= CP helpers =======
    
    /** Get the CPVal for an identifier in the current state */
    private CPVal cpLookup(String var) {
        return cpState.getOrDefault(var, CPVal.UNDEF);
    }
    
    /** Emit a ConstOrId: substitute constant if known, else emit variable name */
    private String constOrId(String varName) {
        CPVal v = cpLookup(varName);
        if (v.isConst()) return v.toSource();
        return varName;
    }
    
    /** Meet two CP states (used at join points) */
    private Map<String, CPVal> meetStates(Map<String, CPVal> s1, Map<String, CPVal> s2) {
        Map<String, CPVal> result = new HashMap<>();
        Set<String> allKeys = new HashSet<>(s1.keySet());
        allKeys.addAll(s2.keySet());
        for (String k : allKeys) {
            CPVal v1 = s1.getOrDefault(k, CPVal.UNDEF);
            CPVal v2 = s2.getOrDefault(k, CPVal.UNDEF);
            result.put(k, CPVal.meet(v1, v2));
        }
        return result;
    }
    
    /** Deep copy a CP state */
    private Map<String, CPVal> copyState(Map<String, CPVal> state) {
        return new HashMap<>(state);
    }
    
    // ======= Expression evaluation =======
    
    /** Evaluate an expression to a CPVal under the current cpState */
    private CPVal evalExpr(Expression e) {
        Node choice = e.f0.choice;
        if (choice instanceof PlusExpression) {
            PlusExpression pe = (PlusExpression) choice;
            CPVal l = cpLookup(pe.f0.f0.tokenImage);
            CPVal r = cpLookup(pe.f2.f0.tokenImage);
            if (l.kind == CPVal.Kind.INT_CONST && r.kind == CPVal.Kind.INT_CONST)
                return CPVal.ofInt(l.intVal + r.intVal);
            return CPVal.NAC;
        }
        if (choice instanceof MinusExpression) {
            MinusExpression me = (MinusExpression) choice;
            CPVal l = cpLookup(me.f0.f0.tokenImage);
            CPVal r = cpLookup(me.f2.f0.tokenImage);
            if (l.kind == CPVal.Kind.INT_CONST && r.kind == CPVal.Kind.INT_CONST)
                return CPVal.ofInt(l.intVal - r.intVal);
            return CPVal.NAC;
        }
        if (choice instanceof TimesExpression) {
            TimesExpression te = (TimesExpression) choice;
            CPVal l = cpLookup(te.f0.f0.tokenImage);
            CPVal r = cpLookup(te.f2.f0.tokenImage);
            if (l.kind == CPVal.Kind.INT_CONST && r.kind == CPVal.Kind.INT_CONST)
                return CPVal.ofInt(l.intVal * r.intVal);
            return CPVal.NAC;
        }
        if (choice instanceof CompareExpression) {
            CompareExpression ce = (CompareExpression) choice;
            CPVal l = cpLookup(ce.f0.f0.tokenImage);
            CPVal r = cpLookup(ce.f2.f0.tokenImage);
            if (l.kind == CPVal.Kind.INT_CONST && r.kind == CPVal.Kind.INT_CONST)
                return CPVal.ofBool(l.intVal < r.intVal);
            return CPVal.NAC;
        }
        if (choice instanceof PrimaryExpression) {
            return evalPrimary((PrimaryExpression) choice);
        }
        // ArrayLookup, ArrayLength, MessageSend, FieldRead → NAC
        return CPVal.NAC;
    }
    
    private CPVal evalPrimary(PrimaryExpression pe) {
        Node choice = pe.f0.choice;
        if (choice instanceof IntegerLiteral) {
            return CPVal.ofInt(Integer.parseInt(((IntegerLiteral)choice).f0.f0.tokenImage));
        }
        if (choice instanceof TrueLiteral)  return CPVal.ofBool(true);
        if (choice instanceof FalseLiteral) return CPVal.ofBool(false);
        if (choice instanceof Identifier) {
            return cpLookup(((Identifier)choice).f0.tokenImage);
        }
        if (choice instanceof NotExpression) {
            CPVal v = cpLookup(((NotExpression)choice).f1.f0.tokenImage);
            if (v.kind == CPVal.Kind.BOOL_CONST) return CPVal.ofBool(!v.boolVal);
            return CPVal.NAC;
        }
        // this, new ..., new int[...] → NAC
        return CPVal.NAC;
    }
    
    // ======= Expression emission (TACoJava2) =======
    
    /** Emit expression as TACoJava2 source, substituting constants in ConstOrId positions.
     *  If the entire expression evaluates to a constant, emit the folded constant as PrimaryExpression. */
    private String emitExpr(Expression e) {
        CPVal val = evalExpr(e);
        if (val.isConst()) {
            return val.toSource(); // fold to constant
        }
        
        Node choice = e.f0.choice;
        if (choice instanceof PlusExpression) {
            PlusExpression pe = (PlusExpression) choice;
            return constOrId(pe.f0.f0.tokenImage) + " + " + constOrId(pe.f2.f0.tokenImage);
        }
        if (choice instanceof MinusExpression) {
            MinusExpression me = (MinusExpression) choice;
            return constOrId(me.f0.f0.tokenImage) + " - " + constOrId(me.f2.f0.tokenImage);
        }
        if (choice instanceof TimesExpression) {
            TimesExpression te = (TimesExpression) choice;
            return constOrId(te.f0.f0.tokenImage) + " * " + constOrId(te.f2.f0.tokenImage);
        }
        if (choice instanceof CompareExpression) {
            CompareExpression ce = (CompareExpression) choice;
            return constOrId(ce.f0.f0.tokenImage) + " < " + constOrId(ce.f2.f0.tokenImage);
        }
        if (choice instanceof ArrayLookup) {
            ArrayLookup al = (ArrayLookup) choice;
            String arr = al.f0.f0.tokenImage; // array identifier (must stay as identifier)
            String idx = constOrId(al.f2.f0.tokenImage); // index is ConstOrId in TACoJava2
            return arr + "[" + idx + "]";
        }
        if (choice instanceof ArrayLength) {
            ArrayLength al = (ArrayLength) choice;
            return al.f0.f0.tokenImage + ".length";
        }
        if (choice instanceof MessageSend) {
            return emitMessageSend((MessageSend) choice);
        }
        if (choice instanceof FieldRead) {
            FieldRead fr = (FieldRead) choice;
            return fr.f0.f0.tokenImage + "." + fr.f2.f0.tokenImage;
        }
        if (choice instanceof PrimaryExpression) {
            return emitPrimary((PrimaryExpression) choice);
        }
        return "/* unknown expr */";
    }
    
    private String emitPrimary(PrimaryExpression pe) {
        Node choice = pe.f0.choice;
        if (choice instanceof IntegerLiteral) {
            return ((IntegerLiteral)choice).f0.f0.tokenImage;
        }
        if (choice instanceof TrueLiteral)  return "true";
        if (choice instanceof FalseLiteral) return "false";
        if (choice instanceof Identifier) {
            return constOrId(((Identifier)choice).f0.tokenImage);
        }
        if (choice instanceof ThisExpression) return "this";
        if (choice instanceof ArrayAllocationExpression) {
            ArrayAllocationExpression ae = (ArrayAllocationExpression) choice;
            // TACoJava2: new int [ ConstOrId ]
            return "new int[" + constOrId(ae.f3.f0.tokenImage) + "]";
        }
        if (choice instanceof AllocationExpression) {
            AllocationExpression ae = (AllocationExpression) choice;
            return "new " + ae.f1.f0.tokenImage + "()";
        }
        if (choice instanceof NotExpression) {
            NotExpression ne = (NotExpression) choice;
            CPVal v = cpLookup(ne.f1.f0.tokenImage);
            if (v.isConst()) return v.toSource(); // fold
            return "!" + ne.f1.f0.tokenImage;
        }
        return "/* unknown primary */";
    }
    
    private String emitMessageSend(MessageSend ms) {
        String receiver = emitPrimary(ms.f0);
        String method = ms.f2.f0.tokenImage;
        StringBuilder args = new StringBuilder();
        if (ms.f4.present()) {
            ArgList al = (ArgList) ms.f4.node;
            args.append(constOrId(al.f0.f0.tokenImage));
            for (Enumeration<Node> e = al.f1.elements(); e.hasMoreElements();) {
                ArgRest ar = (ArgRest) e.nextElement();
                args.append(", ").append(constOrId(ar.f1.f0.tokenImage));
            }
        }
        return receiver + "." + method + "(" + args + ")";
    }
    
    // ======= Dead assignment / pure method check =======
    
    /** True if the expression has no side effects */
    private boolean hasSideEffects(Expression e) {
        Node choice = e.f0.choice;
        if (choice instanceof MessageSend) {
            // Side effects depend on purity of the called method
            MessageSend ms = (MessageSend) choice;
            String method = ms.f2.f0.tokenImage;
            // ... (need to resolve callee)
            // For simplicity: MessageSend always has side effects
            // (pure method check will be used for standalone void calls)
            return true;
        }
        return false;
    }
    
    // ======= Main visitors =======
    
    @Override
    public String visit(Goal n, String argu) {
        n.f0.accept(this, argu); // MainClass
        n.f1.accept(this, argu); // TypeDeclarations
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String visit(MainClass n, String argu) {
        currentClass  = n.f1.f0.tokenImage;
        currentMethod = "main";
        
        // Initialize CP state: no params, locals = UNDEF
        cpState = new HashMap<>();
        Map<String, String> vars = st.methodVars
            .getOrDefault(currentClass, Collections.emptyMap())
            .getOrDefault("main", Collections.emptyMap());
        for (String v : vars.keySet()) cpState.put(v, CPVal.UNDEF);
        
        // Compute non-substitutable vars for this method
        nonSubstitutableVars = findNonSubstitutableVars(currentClass, "main", n.f14, n.f15);
        
        emitln("class " + currentClass + " {");
        indent++;
        emitln(ind() + "public static void main(String [] " + n.f11.f0.tokenImage + ") {");
        indent++;
        
        // Emit var declarations
        emitVarDeclarations(n.f14);
        
        // Emit statements
        n.f15.accept(this, argu);
        
        indent--;
        emitln(ind() + "}");
        indent--;
        emitln("}");
        nl();
        
        currentClass = currentMethod = null;
        return null;
    }
    
    // ... (continue with other visit methods)
}
```

```java
/** Variables used in positions that can't be replaced by constants in TACoJava2 */
class NonSubstitutableVisitor extends GJDepthFirst<Object, Object> {
    Set<String> vars = new HashSet<>();
    
    @Override
    public Object visit(IfStatement n, Object argu) {
        vars.add(n.f2.f0.tokenImage); // condition is Identifier (not ConstOrId)
        n.f4.accept(this, argu);
        n.f6.accept(this, argu);
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Object visit(WhileStatement n, Object argu) {
        vars.add(n.f2.f0.tokenImage); // condition is Identifier (not ConstOrId)
        n.f4.accept(this, argu);
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Object visit(ArrayAssignmentStatement n, Object argu) {
        vars.add(n.f0.f0.tokenImage); // array base is Identifier
        // n.f2 (index) and n.f5 (value) are ConstOrId in TACoJava2 → substitutable
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Object visit(ArrayLookup n, Object argu) {
        vars.add(n.f0.f0.tokenImage); // array base is Identifier
        // n.f2 is ConstOrId in TACoJava2 → substitutable
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Object visit(ArrayLength n, Object argu) {
        vars.add(n.f0.f0.tokenImage); // array is Identifier
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Object visit(FieldRead n, Object argu) {
        vars.add(n.f0.f0.tokenImage); // object receiver is Identifier
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Object visit(FieldAssignmentStatement n, Object argu) {
        vars.add(n.f0.f0.tokenImage); // object receiver is Identifier
        // n.f4 is ConstOrId in TACoJava2 → substitutable
        return null;
    }
    
    // For MessageSend: receiver is PrimaryExpression which can be Identifier
    // We need to check if the PrimaryExpression is an identifier
    @Override
    public Object visit(MessageSend n, Object argu) {
        // Check if receiver is an identifier
        Node choice = n.f0.f0.choice;
        if (choice instanceof Identifier) {
            vars.add(((Identifier)choice).f0.tokenImage);
        }
        n.f4.accept(this, argu); // args
        return null;
```
```java
    }
```
```java
}
```

Let me be very careful about one thing: TACoJava's grammar uses `Identifier()` in certain positions, but TACoJava2 uses `ConstOrId()` in those same positions. The key positions where ConstOrId replaces Identifier are:

TACoJava → TACoJava2:
- In AssignmentStatement: `Identifier() "=" Expression() ";"` → same (both have Expression on RHS)
- In ArrayAssignmentStatement: `Identifier() "[" Identifier() "]" "=" Identifier() ";"` → `Identifier() "[" ConstOrId() "]" "=" ConstOrId() ";"`
- In FieldAssignmentStatement: `Identifier() "." Identifier() "=" Identifier() ";"` → `Identifier() "." Identifier() "=" ConstOrId() ";"`
- In PrintStatement: `"System.out.println" "(" Identifier() ")" ";"` → `"System.out.println" "(" ConstOrId() ")" ";"`
- In CompareExpression: `Identifier() "<" Identifier()` → `ConstOrId() "<" ConstOrId()`
- In PlusExpression: `Identifier() "+" Identifier()` → `ConstOrId() "+" ConstOrId()`
- In MinusExpression: `Identifier() "-" Identifier()` → `ConstOrId() "-" ConstOrId()`
- In TimesExpression: `Identifier() "*" Identifier()` → `ConstOrId() "*" ConstOrId()`
- In ArrayLookup: `Identifier() "[" Identifier() "]"` → `Identifier() "[" ConstOrId() "]"` (array base stays Identifier)
- In ArgList: `Identifier() (ArgRest())*` → `ConstOrId() (ArgRest())*`
- In ArgRest: `"," Identifier()` → `"," ConstOrId()`
- In MethodDeclaration return: `"return" Identifier() ";"` → `"return" ConstOrId() ";"`
- In ArrayAllocationExpression: `"new" "int" "[" Identifier() "]"` → `"new" "int" "[" ConstOrId() "]"`

And the key positions that REMAIN as Identifier() (can NOT be substituted):
- IfStatement condition: `"if" "(" Identifier() ")"` → same in TACoJava2
- WhileStatement condition: `"while" "(" Identifier() ")"` → same in TACoJava2
- ArrayAssignmentStatement LHS array: `Identifier() "[..."` → same (first identifier stays)
- ArrayLookup/ArrayLength base: `Identifier() "[..."` or `Identifier() "."` → same
- FieldRead receiver: `Identifier() "." Identifier()` → same
- FieldAssignmentStatement receiver: `Identifier() "." Identifier() "="` → same (first identifier stays)
- MessageSend receiver: `PrimaryExpression() "."` → same (identifier still valid as PrimaryExpression)
- ForStatement init/update LHS: same

For LoopVarFinder: a simple visitor that collects all LHS variables in AssignmentStatements within the loop body.

```java
class LoopVarFinder extends GJDepthFirst<Object, Object> {
    Set<String> assignedVars = new HashSet<>();
    
    @Override
    public Object visit(AssignmentStatement n, Object argu) {
        assignedVars.add(n.f0.f0.tokenImage); // LHS identifier
        // Don't recurse into sub-expressions (but there are none in TACoJava anyway)
        return null;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Object visit(ForStatement n, Object argu) {
        assignedVars.add(n.f2.f0.tokenImage); // init LHS
        assignedVars.add(n.f8.f0.tokenImage); // update LHS
        n.f12.accept(this, argu); // body
        return null;
```
```java
    }
```
```java
}
```

Here's the complete plan for CodeGenVisitor:

```
Per method:
1. Compute nonSubstitutableVars (scan whole method body for vars in Identifier-only positions)
2. Initialize cpState: params=NAC, locals=UNDEF
3. Process statements in order:
   - AssignmentStatement "x = expr":
     a. val = evalExpr(expr)
     b. isDead = (val.isConst() || cpLookup(x).isConst()) && !nonSubstitutableVars.contains(x) && !hasSideEffects(expr)
     c. cpState[x] = val
     d. If not dead: emit "x = emitExpr(expr);"

   - IfStatement "if (c) S1 else S2":
     a. cVal = cpLookup(c)
     b. If cVal.isTrue(): emit S1 (process S1 with current cpState)
     c. If cVal.isFalse(): emit S2
     d. Else: save state, process S1 → state1, restore state, process S2 → state2, merge state1 and state2
   - WhileStatement "while (c) S":
     a. cVal = cpLookup(c)
     b. If cVal.isFalse(): skip (emit nothing)
     c. Else: kill all vars written in S body, emit "while (c) { emitS(S) }"
   - PrintStatement "System.out.println(x)":
     emit "System.out.println(constOrId(x));"
   - ForStatement: kill conservatively, emit with substitutions in expressions
   - Block: recurse
   - ArrayAssignmentStatement: emit with constOrId substitutions
   - FieldAssignmentStatement: emit with constOrId substitution in value
```

All variable read positions (in TACoJava):
- Arithmetic expressions operands (PlusExpression, MinusExpression, etc.)
- CompareExpression operands
- ArrayLookup: array[index] → both array and index are reads
- ArrayLength: array.length → array is a read
- FieldRead: obj.field → obj is a read
- FieldAssignmentStatement: obj.field = val → obj and val are reads, field is special
- ArrayAssignmentStatement: arr[idx] = val → arr, idx, val are reads
- PrintStatement: println(x) → x is a read
- IfStatement condition: if (c) → c is a read
- WhileStatement condition: while (c) → c is a read
- MessageSend receiver (in PrimaryExpression if it's an Identifier) and args
- return x → x is a read
- NotExpression: !x → x is a read
- Assignment RHS in general: all variables appearing in the expression

Let me implement: `isCallPure(MessageSend ms)` which checks if all CHA-resolved callees are in pureMethods.

```java
private boolean isCallPure(MessageSend ms) {
    String receiverType = getPrimaryType(ms.f0);
    String methodName = ms.f2.f0.tokenImage;
    if (receiverType == null) return false;
    
    Set<String> candidates = new HashSet<>();
    candidates.add(receiverType);
    candidates.addAll(st.allSubclasses(receiverType));
    
    for (String cls : candidates) {
        String defClass = st.resolveMethod(cls, methodName);
        if (defClass != null) {
            String calleeId = defClass + "." + methodName;
            if (!pureMethods.contains(calleeId)) return false; // at least one impure callee
        }
    }
    return true; // all callees (if any) are pure
}
```

```java
private String typeToSource(Type t) {
    Node choice = t.f0.choice;
    if (choice instanceof ArrayType) return "int []";
    if (choice instanceof BooleanType) return "boolean";
    if (choice instanceof IntegerType) return "int";
    if (choice instanceof Identifier) return ((Identifier)choice).f0.tokenImage;
    return "/* unknown type */";
}
```

```java
private String emitFormalParams(NodeOptional fpl) {
    if (!fpl.present()) return "";
    FormalParameterList list = (FormalParameterList) fpl.node;
    StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
    sb.append(emitFormalParam(list.f0)); // first param
    for (Enumeration<Node> e = list.f1.elements(); e.hasMoreElements();) {
        FormalParameterRest rest = (FormalParameterRest) e.nextElement();
        sb.append(", ").append(emitFormalParam(rest.f1));
    }
    return sb.toString();
}

private String emitFormalParam(FormalParameter fp) {
    return typeToSource(fp.f0) + " " + fp.f1.f0.tokenImage;
```
```java
}
```

Here's what was built and the key design decisions to keep in mind as we proceed:

---

### `ClassHierarchyVisitor` — what it does

- Visits `MainClass`, `ClassDeclaration`, `ClassExtendsDeclaration`, and `MethodDeclaration`.
- Builds three core maps: `parentOf`, `childrenOf`, `methodsOf`.
- Exposes `allSubclasses(C)` (BFS transitive closure) — this is what CHA will call in Pass 3.
- Exposes `declaringClass(C, m)` — walks up the ancestor chain to find who actually defines method `m`, which you'll need when CHA resolves virtual dispatch.

### `SymbolTableVisitor` — what it does

- Uses `currentClass`/`currentMethod` as context while walking.
- `VarDeclaration` nodes go to **fields** if `currentMethod == null`, **locals** otherwise.
- `FormalParameter` nodes go into locals AND the ordered `paramOrder` list (you'll need param order later when evaluating call arguments).
- The main class's local vars are stored under the synthetic method name `"main"`.
- `typeOf(class, method, var)` implements the correct lookup order: locals → own fields → inherited fields.

---

### One thing to confirm before we proceed to the CallGraph (Pass 3)

In `GJDepthFirst`, the type parameter notation from the file is `GJDepthFirst<Object, Object>` — meaning both the return type `R` and argument type `A` are `Object`. Our visitors use `Object` for both, which matches. Just make sure when you run JTB that the generated `GJDepthFirst` has those same type params.

Ready to move to **Pass 3: CallGraphVisitor** (CHA)?

Here's what was built and why each decision was made:

---

### Structure: Two-phase design

The visitor does two passes in a single `visit(Goal)` call:

**Phase 1 — Indexing:** Walks the entire AST and stores each `MethodDeclaration` node in `methodIndex[className][methodName]`. This is necessary because we need to scan method *bodies* during propagation, and we need all methods indexed before we start so we can look up callees that haven't been visited yet. The `MainClass` node is captured separately since its statements aren't wrapped in a `MethodDeclaration`.

**Phase 2 — Worklist propagation:** Classic BFS. For each reachable method we scan its body with the inner `MessageSendCollector`, then CHA-resolve each call site.

---

### CHA resolution in `resolveReceiverType`

The receiver of a call `x.foo()` in TACoJava is always a `PrimaryExpression`. We handle the three useful cases:
- **`Identifier`** → look up declared type in the symbol table
- **`this`** → type is the enclosing class
- **`new Foo()`** → type is `Foo` directly (most precise possible)

Everything else (primitives, arrays) returns `null` and is skipped — those can't have user-defined methods called on them in a valid program.

---

### One subtlety: `lookupMethod` walks the hierarchy

When we look up a callee's `MethodDeclaration` to scan its body, we walk up the inheritance chain. This handles the case where a subclass inherits a method from a parent without overriding it — we still find and scan the parent's body.

---

Ready to move to **Pass 4: ConstPropVisitor**? That's the most complex one — it implements the lattice, intraprocedural dataflow, and conditional CP.

Let's build **Pass 4: ConstPropVisitor**. This is the most complex pass — we'll break it into two files: the lattice value type, and the visitor itself.
```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```

Now the main visitor:
```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```


```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```

Here's the key design rationale for each tricky part:

---

### `CPValue` — the lattice

Three levels: `UNDEF` (bottom) → `CONST(v)` → `NAC` (top). The meet operation is the heart of it — `UNDEF` is the identity element (contributes nothing at a join point), same two constants stay constant, different constants collapse to `NAC`. We store the actual Java `Integer` or `Boolean` in `value` so arithmetic can be folded directly.

---

### Fixpoint iteration strategy

There are two levels of iteration:

1. **`analyseMethod`** calls `iterateStatements` which loops the whole statement list until the state map stops changing. This handles the case where a later statement affects an earlier one (e.g. through a loop back-edge).
2. **`visit(WhileStatement)`** also has its own inner fixpoint loop for the body. This is correct — the outer loop converges because the lattice only moves upward (UNDEF → CONST → NAC), so it terminates in at most `|vars|` iterations.

---

### Conditional CP — the key insight

In `visit(IfStatement)`: when `condVal.isTrue()` we only visit the then-branch, never touching the else-branch state. This means variables assigned only in the dead branch correctly stay as they were before the if. When both branches are live, we snapshot `state` before each branch, run them independently, then `meetMaps` the two results — this is the standard flow-join.

---

### Fields are always NAC

`isField()` walks up the hierarchy. Any write through any alias could change a field, so we never track field values in the state map — we return `NAC` immediately on any field read. This is sound and the right choice for context-insensitive analysis.

---

### What the CodeGen pass will use

- `getVarValue(cls, method, var)` — to decide whether to emit a constant or the identifier
- `getExprValue(node)` — recorded for each `Expression` node, so CodeGen can check if the whole RHS folded to a constant
- `exprValues` on `PrintStatement` nodes — so CodeGen knows if the print arg is a known constant

Ready to move to **Pass 5: DeadCodeVisitor**?

chat_messages.text:
Let's build **Pass 5: DeadCodeVisitor** — liveness analysis + dead code marking.
```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```


```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```

Here's the key design rationale for every tricky part:

---

### Liveness is computed backwards — but stored per-statement

`analyseMethod` builds an array `liveOut[i]` for each statement in the method body. It runs a backward fixpoint loop over the whole statement list — needed because a loop back-edge makes a later statement's `liveOut` depend on an earlier statement's `liveIn`. The lattice here is just sets of variable names (monotonically growing under union), so termination is guaranteed.

---

### `liveIn` — the transfer function

For each statement kind the rule is `liveIn = (liveOut − defs) ∪ uses`:

- **AssignmentStatement `x = expr`**: remove `x` from live (it's defined here), add all vars used in `expr`.
- **IfStatement**: if the condition is a known constant, only the live branch contributes; otherwise we take the **union** of both branches' `liveIn` sets (conservative — either branch might execute).
- **WhileStatement with `false` condition**: body contributes nothing (never executes).
- **Block**: backward-threads through its inner statement list.

---

### Dead assignment rule — two conditions must both hold

```
x = expr  is dead  iff  x ∉ liveOut  AND  expr has no side effects
```

`hasSideEffects` checks only for `MessageSend` — that's the only expression form in TACoJava that can mutate program state or produce I/O. `new`, arithmetic, array length etc. are all pure.

---

### Unreachable branch marking

`markDeadStatement` consults `cp.getVarValue(cls, method, condName)` for `if` and `while` nodes. The result goes into `deadIfBranch` (a map from node → `"then"` or `"else"`) and `deadWhiles` (a set). The CodeGen pass will query these to decide what to emit.

---

### Dead methods — simplest case

`markDeadMethods` just asks the call graph: for every `(class, method)` pair known from the class hierarchy, if `cg.reachable()` is false, it's dead. CodeGen will skip the entire `MethodDeclaration`.

---

Ready for **Pass 6: CodeGenVisitor** — the final pretty-printing pass that ties everything together?

Let's build the final pass — the **CodeGenVisitor** that pretty-prints TACoJava2 to stdout.
```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```

Now let's write `P3.java` to wire everything together:
```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```


```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```

Here's the full rationale for every non-obvious decision:

---

### The `emitStatement` / `NodeListOptional` interception pattern

This is the central trick. Rather than overriding `visit(Statement)` — which JTB calls for every statement node including those inside `if`/`while` bodies — we override `visit(NodeListOptional)` to intercept statement *lists* specifically. Every statement list in TACoJava (`MainClass.f15`, `MethodDeclaration.f8`, `Block.f1`) is a `NodeListOptional`, so this one override catches all of them. Individual statements inside `if`/`while` bodies are **not** in a `NodeListOptional` — they go through the normal visitor dispatch, which is correct since we handle their dead-branch logic in `visit(IfStatement)` and `visit(WhileStatement)`.

---

### Dead assignment → VoidMessageSendStatement promotion

When an assignment `x = obj.foo(args)` is dead (x is never read), we can't just drop it — `foo` might have side effects. So in `emitStatement`, when `dc.isDead()` is true but the RHS is a `MessageSend`, we emit `obj.foo(args);` as a `VoidMessageSendStatement` — a new statement form that TACoJava2 adds precisely for this purpose. Pure RHS dead assignments are dropped silently.

---

### `constOrId` — the substitution core

Every place TACoJava2 allows a `ConstOrId` (operands of arithmetic, print arg, return value, array index, field RHS, call args) runs through this single helper. It asks `cp.getIdValue(cls, method, name)` — which returns NAC for fields (never substitute), NAC for parameters (context-insensitive), and the constant value for provably constant locals. `CPValue.toTokenString()` handles signed integers (`-3`) and booleans correctly.

---

### Dead branch emission for `if`

`dc.getDeadBranch(n)` returns `"else"` when the condition was always `true` (meaning the else branch is dead — we keep only then), and `"then"` when the condition was always `false` (keep only else). The naming is slightly counter-intuitive but consistent: **the string names the dead branch**, not the live one.

---

### Complete file list

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `CPValue.java` | Lattice value for CP |
| `ClassHierarchyVisitor.java` | Pass 1 — hierarchy + CHA support |
| `SymbolTableVisitor.java` | Pass 2 — type info per scope |
| `CallGraphVisitor.java` | Pass 3 — CHA reachability |
| `ConstPropVisitor.java` | Pass 4 — conditional CP |
| `DeadCodeVisitor.java` | Pass 5 — liveness + dead marking |
| `CodeGenVisitor.java` | Pass 6 — TACoJava2 emission |
| `P3.java` | Main — wires all passes |

To compile and run after setting up JTB/JavaCC:
```bash
jtb tacojava.jj
javacc jtb.out.jj
javac *.java visitor/*.java
java P3 < P.java > Pc.java
```

This is the central trick. Rather than overriding `visit(Statement)` — which JTB calls for every statement node including those inside `if`/`while` bodies — we override `visit(NodeListOptional)` to intercept statement *lists* specifically. Every statement list in TACoJava (`MainClass.f15`, `MethodDeclaration.f8`, `Block.f1`) is a `NodeListOptional`, so this one override catches all of them. Individual statements inside `if`/`while` bodies are **not** in a `NodeListOptional` — they go through the normal visitor dispatch, which is correct since we handle their dead-branch logic in `visit(IfStatement)` and `visit(WhileStatement)`.

---

### Dead assignment → VoidMessageSendStatement promotion

When an assignment `x = obj.foo(args)` is dead (x is never read), we can't just drop it — `foo` might have side effects. So in `emitStatement`, when `dc.isDead()` is true but the RHS is a `MessageSend`, we emit `obj.foo(args);` as a `VoidMessageSendStatement` — a new statement form that TACoJava2 adds precisely for this purpose. Pure RHS dead assignments are dropped silently.

---

### `constOrId` — the substitution core

Every place TACoJava2 allows a `ConstOrId` (operands of arithmetic, print arg, return value, array index, field RHS, call args) runs through this single helper. It asks `cp.getIdValue(cls, method, name)` — which returns NAC for fields (never substitute), NAC for parameters (context-insensitive), and the constant value for provably constant locals. `CPValue.toTokenString()` handles signed integers (`-3`) and booleans correctly.

---

### Dead branch emission for `if`

`dc.getDeadBranch(n)` returns `"else"` when the condition was always `true` (meaning the else branch is dead — we keep only then), and `"then"` when the condition was always `false` (keep only else). The naming is slightly counter-intuitive but consistent: **the string names the dead branch**, not the live one.

---

### Complete file list

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `CPValue.java` | Lattice value for CP |
| `ClassHierarchyVisitor.java` | Pass 1 — hierarchy + CHA support |
| `SymbolTableVisitor.java` | Pass 2 — type info per scope |
| `CallGraphVisitor.java` | Pass 3 — CHA reachability |
| `ConstPropVisitor.java` | Pass 4 — conditional CP |
| `DeadCodeVisitor.java` | Pass 5 — liveness + dead marking |
| `CodeGenVisitor.java` | Pass 6 — TACoJava2 emission |
| `P3.java` | Main — wires all passes |

To compile and run after setting up JTB/JavaCC:
```bash
jtb tacojava.jj
javacc jtb.out.jj
javac *.java visitor/*.java
java P3 < P.java > Pc.java
```

Here's a precise account of every bug fixed:

---

### Bug 1 — `ConstPropVisitor`: overwrite vs meet (root cause of CP not folding)

The old code did `state.put(lhs, CPValue.meet(old, rhs))` for every assignment. This is wrong for straight-line code. Consider:
```
x = 4;   // state[x] = meet(UNDEF, CONST(4)) = CONST(4)  ✓ first pass
```
But on the **second fixpoint iteration** (triggered by presence of any loop) it would do `meet(CONST(4), CONST(4)) = CONST(4)` — that's fine. The actual break was subtler: `y = x * b` computes `meet(UNDEF, CONST(8))` correctly on pass 1, but if `y` was previously CONST(8) and got re-evaluated, it was already fine. The real bug was that **the fixpoint loop re-initialized state to UNDEF each time** by re-running `initLocals` — which reset already-computed values. Now the fix is clean: **assignments just overwrite** (`state.put(lhs, rhs)`). Meet only happens explicitly in `visit(IfStatement)` and `visit(WhileStatement)` at actual join points.

### Bug 2 — `CodeGenVisitor`: dead branch block unwrapping

Old code called `emitStatement(is.f4)` for the surviving branch, which hit `emitBlock()` and emitted `{ x = 5; }`. The new `emitBranchBody()` checks if the surviving branch unwraps to a `Block`, and if so emits its contents directly with `emitStatementList` — no extra braces.

### Bug 3 — `CodeGenVisitor`: VarDeclaration routing

Separated `emitVarDeclList()` (for `f14`, `f7`, `f3`, `f5`) from `emitStatementList()`. Previously both went through `visit(NodeListOptional)` which was overridden to intercept statements — meaning var decls were accidentally routed through statement dead-code logic.

---

**Test 2:** `flag=true` → if-branch eliminated, block unwrapped, `flag=true` dropped as dead, `System.out.println(5);` emitted directly.

chat_messages.text:
The dead assignment elimination is not working because liveness analysis isn't seeing these variables as dead. The issue is that `DeadCodeVisitor.getVarValue` is being called but CP stores the final state — liveness needs to see that after `System.out.println(10)` (with constant substitution), none of `x, b, y, cond, z` are ever read.

The root cause: **liveness uses `cp.getVarValue` to check conditions, but `constOrId` in CodeGen already substituted the print arg to `10` — liveness still sees `z` as used in the print statement** because it looks at the AST identifier `z`, not the substituted constant.

The fix: in `DeadCodeVisitor`, when computing uses of a `PrintStatement` or any identifier-use position, **if CP says that identifier is a constant, it contributes NO uses** — a substituted identifier is no longer a real use.

1. **`PrintStatement`** — `z` added as live even if CP says it's constant
2. **`usesOfExpression`** — every identifier in every expression added as live even if constant

6. **Return variable in `exitLive`** — added even if it will be substituted

The fix is a single helper `isLiveId(name, cls, method)` — returns false if CP says this identifier will be substituted with a constant.

Here's exactly what was wrong and why the fix is correct:

---

### The root cause: liveness was CP-unaware

The old liveness treated every identifier that appears in the source as a live use. But after CP, many of those identifiers will be **replaced by literal constants** in the output — so they are never actually read at runtime.

Consider the chain:
```
x = 4;              // CP: x = CONST(4)
b = 2;              // CP: b = CONST(2)
y = x * b;          // CP: y = CONST(8), and CodeGen emits "y = 4 * 2"
                    //     but x and b are substituted -> x and b have NO real uses
z = y + b;          // CP: z = CONST(10), y and b substituted -> no real uses
System.out.println(z);  // CP: z = CONST(10), substituted -> z has NO real use
```

With the old code, `println(z)` added `z` to the live set, making `z = y + b` live, which added `y` and `b`, making `y = x * b` live, and so on — nothing was dead.

---

### The fix: `addIfLive`

One small helper replaces every bare `live.add(varName)` at identifier-use positions:

```java
private void addIfLive(Set<String> liveSet, String varName, String cls, String method) {
    CPValue val = cp.getIdValue(cls, method, varName);
    if (!val.isConst())
        liveSet.add(varName);
}
```

`cp.getIdValue` already handles the field-vs-local distinction. If the result is `CONST`, CodeGen will emit the literal — the variable is not a real use. If it's `NAC` or `UNDEF`, the variable IS read at runtime — it stays live.

---

### Where `addIfLive` is applied vs where raw `.add` is kept

- **`addIfLive`**: `PrintStatement` arg, arithmetic operands, array index, field-assign value, array-assign index/value, message-send args, `if`/`while` condition, return variable at exit
- **Raw `.add` kept**: message-send **receiver** object, array object in `ArrayLookup`/`ArrayLength`/`ArrayAssignment`, field-read object — these are object references, not scalar values, so they can never be constant-folded to a literal in TACoJava2

Now for your test case, tracing through:
- `println(z)` → `z` is `CONST(10)` → `addIfLive` does NOT add `z` → `z` has no live-out → `z = y + b` is dead (and its RHS `y+b` has no side effects)
- Similarly `y`, `x`, `b`, `cond` are all dead-assigned
- Final output should be just `System.out.println(10);` with the var declarations still present (we never drop declarations, only assignments)

content.text:
The dead assignment elimination is not working because liveness analysis isn't seeing these variables as dead. The issue is that `DeadCodeVisitor.getVarValue` is being called but CP stores the final state — liveness needs to see that after `System.out.println(10)` (with constant substitution), none of `x, b, y, cond, z` are ever read.

The root cause: **liveness uses `cp.getVarValue` to check conditions, but `constOrId` in CodeGen already substituted the print arg to `10` — liveness still sees `z` as used in the print statement** because it looks at the AST identifier `z`, not the substituted constant.

The fix: in `DeadCodeVisitor`, when computing uses of a `PrintStatement` or any identifier-use position, **if CP says that identifier is a constant, it contributes NO uses** — a substituted identifier is no longer a real use.

Here's a precise account of every fix:

---

### Fix 1 — `markDeadStatementRecursive` replaces flat `markDeadStatement`

The old `markDeadStatement` was only called on the **flat top-level list** of a method. So `z = y + b` inside the else-branch of the if-statement was never visited for dead-marking — it had no entry in the `liveOut` array and was never checked.

The new `markDeadStatementRecursive` recurses into every nested body:
- **`IfStatement`**: recurses into both then/else branches, passing `liveOut` of the whole if as the liveOut for each branch (both branches exit to the same successor point)
- **`WhileStatement`**: recurses into the body with `liveOut ∪ uses(cond)` as the body's liveOut (conservative — after the body, the condition is re-evaluated)
- **`Block`**: calls `recurseIntoBlock` which does a fresh backward scan through the block's statement list to get accurate per-statement `liveOut` values, then recurses into each

---

### Fix 2 — `everLive` tracking for dead var declarations

After the backward fixpoint, we take the **union of all `liveOut[i]` sets** across every statement in the method. Any variable never appearing in any of these sets is never read as a non-constant anywhere — so its `VarDeclaration` can be dropped.

`isDeadVarDecl(cls, method, name)` returns true iff `name` is absent from the ever-live set.

`CodeGenVisitor.visit(VarDeclaration)` now calls this before emitting — field declarations (`currentMethod == null`) are always kept since they're class-level state.

---

```java
}
```

All of `x, b, y, cond, z` are CP-substituted constants — never live as variables — declarations and assignments all dropped.

This is **interprocedural constant propagation** — propagating constants across call boundaries when call sites provide known constant arguments.

Let me think through the design carefully before coding.

---

### Design

**What we need:**
- For each method, for each parameter, collect the **meet of all argument values** passed at every call site
- If a parameter always receives the same constant across all call sites → treat it as `CONST` instead of `NAC` in CP
- After re-running CP with the tighter parameter values, dead methods / assignments fall out naturally from the existing passes

**Key constraint:** Still context-insensitive — we compute one summary per method (meet of all call sites), not one per call site.

**Call site argument collection:**
- Scan all reachable methods for `MessageSend` nodes
- For each call `x.foo(a, b)`, resolve via CHA to find target methods
- For each argument position, look up the CP value of the argument identifier at the call site
- Meet that value into the parameter's summary

**Iteration:** After updating parameter summaries, re-run CP — new constants may enable new dead assignments which may expose new constant arguments at other call sites. Repeat until fixpoint.

**Order of passes changes:**
```
Old:  CHA → CP (params=NAC) → DeadCode → CodeGen
New:  CHA → InterCP (iterate: arg collection + CP) → DeadCode → CodeGen
```

Here's the full design rationale:

---

### What changed in `ConstPropVisitor`

Two additions:

**`paramSummaries` map** — stores `"cls::method" → paramName → CPValue`. In `analyseMethod`, instead of always initializing parameters to `NAC`, we now call `getParamSummary(cls, method, param)`, which returns the injected value if one exists, or `NAC` as the safe default.

**`reAnalyse()`** — clears `methodStates`, re-runs `analyseMethod` for all reachable methods with current summaries, returns `true` if anything changed. This is what `InterConstPropVisitor` calls each iteration.

---

### How `InterConstPropVisitor` works

**`buildCallSiteIndex`** (once): scans every reachable method body and collects all `MessageSend` nodes, grouped by caller. This is a one-time scan — the AST doesn't change.

**`collectAndInjectArguments`** (each iteration):
- For each call site `x.foo(a, b)` in each reachable caller:
  - Resolve `x`'s type via the same CHA logic as `CallGraphVisitor`
  - Look up each argument's `CPValue` from the **caller's current CP state** (not NAC — it's the refined value after the latest `reAnalyse`)
  - For each CHA-resolved target method, meet the argument values into `newSummaries`
- Any method with no call sites targeting it gets `NAC` (could be called from outside the program)
- Inject everything into `cpv.setParamSummary`

**Fixpoint loop**: `collectAndInjectArguments` → `cpv.reAnalyse()` → repeat until `reAnalyse` returns `false` (no state changed). Terminates because CPValues only move upward in the lattice (UNDEF → CONST → NAC).

---

### For your example

```java
int foo(int a) { return a + 2; }
// called only as: foo(2)
```

**Iteration 1:** initial CP has `a=NAC`. Call site has `a=CONST(2)`. Summary: `a=CONST(2)`. `reAnalyse` sets `a=CONST(2)` → `return a+2` folds to `CONST(4)`. State changed → iterate.

**Iteration 2:** No new constants discovered. `reAnalyse` returns `false` → done.

Then `DeadCodeVisitor` sees `foo` has no uses of `a` as a non-constant, the return value is substituted, and if the call site's result is also unused, `foo` gets eliminated entirely by `isDeadMethod` (it may still be reachable per CHA — but if the assignment `x = obj.foo(2)` has `x` dead and no side effects beyond return, it's dead too).

Here's the complete account of every change and why:

---

### Fix 1 — `ConstPropVisitor.evalExpression` now folds `MessageSend`

The new `evalMessageSend` method:
1. Resolves the receiver's declared type using the current method context
2. Calls `getReturnValue(receiverType, methodName)` which looks up the return variable's CP value across all CHA targets and returns their meet
3. If all targets return the same constant → the call site folds to that constant

This means in `main`, `result = obj.live(5)` evaluates to `CONST(7)` (since `live` always returns `7`), so `result` becomes `CONST(7)` in main's CP state, and `System.out.println(result)` substitutes to `System.out.println(7)`.

---

### Fix 2 — `markDeadMethods` eliminates constant-return pure methods

A reachable method is now also marked dead if:
- `cp.getReturnValue(cls, method).isConst()` — all CHA targets return the same constant
- `!cp.methodHasSideEffects(cls, method)` — body has no `println` or field writes

`live` satisfies both → it's dead. `dead` was already dead (unreachable).

---

### Fix 3 — `hasSideEffectsToKeep` replaces the old `hasSideEffects` for assignment dead-marking

The old rule: any `MessageSend` RHS = has side effects = keep the call.

The new rule: if **all CHA targets of the call are constant-return and pure** → the call has no observable effect → drop the assignment entirely (don't promote to void call).

`dropMessageSendCall` in `DeadCodeVisitor` implements this check, and `CodeGenVisitor.emitAssignment` consults it — if `dropMessageSendCall` returns true, the dead assignment is silently dropped rather than promoted to a void call statement.

---

### Fix 4 — liveness: receiver/args of a pure call are not live uses

In `usesOfExpression`, when a `MessageSend` is a pure-constant callee (`isCalleeConstantAndPure`), we don't add the receiver or arguments to the live set — since the entire call will be substituted by a constant literal, neither the receiver object nor the argument values are actually read.

---

### Expected output for your test case

```java
   public static void main(String [] a) {
      System.out.println(7);
   }
}
class A {
}
```

`live` is eliminated (constant return `7`, no side effects). `dead` was already eliminated (unreachable). Class `A` itself stays because dropping classes isn't in scope — but it will be empty.

content.text:
Two separate issues:

**Problem 1:** In `main`, `result = obj.live(5)` — the return value of `live` is `CONST(7)` from interprocedural CP, but `result` in `main` is still showing as `result` instead of `7`, and `result = obj.live(5)` isn't being eliminated. The issue is that `MessageSend` return values are always `NAC` in `evalExpression` — we need to look up the callee's return variable's CP value.

**Problem 2:** `live` is reachable per CHA (it's called), so it's not marked dead. But after interprocedural CP, if the return value is a constant AND the call result is always substituted, we can treat the method as eliminatable. This requires a new "pure constant return" check.

Let me trace exactly what needs to change:

Now I have a complete picture. Here are all the issues:

**Problem 1:** `evalExpression` returns `CPValue.NAC` for `MessageSend`. We need it to look up the callee's return variable's CP value — if the callee always returns a constant, the call site should fold to that constant.

**Problem 2:** A method should be eliminated when ALL of these hold:
- Its return value is always a constant (CP-foldable at every call site)
- It has no observable side effects (no `PrintStatement` or field mutations anywhere in its body)
- Every call to it is either a dead assignment or the LHS gets constant-substituted

The clean solution: add `getReturnValue(cls, method)` to `ConstPropVisitor`, add `hasSideEffects(cls, method)` for body inspection, and extend `DeadCodeVisitor` to mark methods dead when their return is a constant AND they have no side effects.

content.text:
Here's the complete account of every change and why:

---

### Fix 1 — `ConstPropVisitor.evalExpression` now folds `MessageSend`

The new `evalMessageSend` method:
1. Resolves the receiver's declared type using the current method context
2. Calls `getReturnValue(receiverType, methodName)` which looks up the return variable's CP value across all CHA targets and returns their meet
3. If all targets return the same constant → the call site folds to that constant

This means in `main`, `result = obj.live(5)` evaluates to `CONST(7)` (since `live` always returns `7`), so `result` becomes `CONST(7)` in main's CP state, and `System.out.println(result)` substitutes to `System.out.println(7)`.

---

### Fix 2 — `markDeadMethods` eliminates constant-return pure methods

A reachable method is now also marked dead if:
- `cp.getReturnValue(cls, method).isConst()` — all CHA targets return the same constant
- `!cp.methodHasSideEffects(cls, method)` — body has no `println` or field writes

`live` satisfies both → it's dead. `dead` was already dead (unreachable).

---

### Fix 3 — `hasSideEffectsToKeep` replaces the old `hasSideEffects` for assignment dead-marking

The old rule: any `MessageSend` RHS = has side effects = keep the call.

The new rule: if **all CHA targets of the call are constant-return and pure** → the call has no observable effect → drop the assignment entirely (don't promote to void call).

`dropMessageSendCall` in `DeadCodeVisitor` implements this check, and `CodeGenVisitor.emitAssignment` consults it — if `dropMessageSendCall` returns true, the dead assignment is silently dropped rather than promoted to a void call statement.

---

### Fix 4 — liveness: receiver/args of a pure call are not live uses

In `usesOfExpression`, when a `MessageSend` is a pure-constant callee (`isCalleeConstantAndPure`), we don't add the receiver or arguments to the live set — since the entire call will be substituted by a constant literal, neither the receiver object nor the argument values are actually read.

---

### Expected output for your test case

```java
class Main {
   public static void main(String [] a) {
      System.out.println(7);
   }
}
class A {
}
```

`live` is eliminated (constant return `7`, no side effects). `dead` was already eliminated (unreachable). Class `A` itself stays because dropping classes isn't in scope — but it will be empty.

The actual bug was a single method signature mismatch. Here's the precise account:

---

### The real bug: `dropMessageSendCall` had a broken no-context overload

`dropMessageSendCall` is called from `CodeGenVisitor.emitAssignment` to decide whether a dead `x = obj.foo()` assignment should be **dropped silently** (pure callee) or **promoted to a void call** (callee has side effects).

The old signature was `dropMessageSendCall(AssignmentStatement as)` — no caller context. Inside, it called a private `resolveReceiverType(MessageSend ms)` overload that could only handle `AllocationExpression` receivers (e.g. `new A().foo()`). For `Identifier` receivers (e.g. `obj.foo()` where `obj` is a variable), it returned `null` → `dropMessageSendCall` returned `false` (conservative: don't drop) → CodeGen emitted `obj.live(5);` as a void call instead of dropping it.

**Fix:** `dropMessageSendCall` now takes `(AssignmentStatement as, String callerCls, String callerMeth)` and calls the existing full `resolveReceiverType(ms, cls, method)` that handles `Identifier`, `this`, and `AllocationExpression` receivers. `CodeGenVisitor` passes `currentClass` and `currentMethod`.

---

### Full execution trace for your test case (now correct)

1. **`A::live` analysed** (topo-first): `x=CONST(5)` from param summary, `k=CONST(2)`, `r=CONST(7)`, return var `r=CONST(7)`
2. **`Main::main` analysed**: `b=CONST(5)`, `result = evalMessageSend(live)` → `getReturnValue("A","live")` → `CONST(7)`, so `result=CONST(7)`
3. **DeadCodeVisitor**: `result` is `CONST(7)` → never live → `result = obj.live(b)` is dead; `live` is constant+pure → `dropMessageSendCall` returns `true` → **drop entirely** (not promoted)
4. **CodeGen**: `println(result)` → `constOrId("result")` → `"7"` → `System.out.println(7)` ✓

Expected output:
```java
class Main {
   public static void main(String [] a) {
      System.out.println(7);
   }
}
class A {
}
```

Here's the exact explanation of every bug fixed:

---

### Bug 1 (root cause): `markDeadMethods` checked methods individually, not as dispatch groups

The old code: for each method `cls::method`, call `cp.getReturnValue(cls, method)`. For `B.bar`, this expands candidates to just `{B}` (B has no subclasses), finds `B.bar` returns `CONST(9)` → marks `B.bar` dead. This is **semantically wrong** — `B.bar` is a virtual override of `A.bar`, and any call `object.bar()` where `object:A` could dispatch to `B.bar` at runtime. Eliminating `B.bar` while keeping `A.bar` changes program behavior.

The fix: find the **dispatch root** — the topmost ancestor that declares the method. Only process from the root. Call `getReturnValue(root, method)` which CHA-expands to ALL overrides. Only if the entire group returns the same constant AND no member has side effects → mark ALL members of the group dead together.

For this test case:
- `A.foo` dispatch root = `A`. `getReturnValue("A","foo")` = meet(`CONST(4)`, `CONST(4)`) = `CONST(4)` → group is dead → mark `A.foo` and `B.foo` dead ✓
- `A.bar` dispatch root = `A`. `getReturnValue("A","bar")` = meet(`CONST(6)`, `CONST(9)`) = `NAC` → group NOT dead → keep both `A.bar` and `B.bar` ✓

---

### Bug 2: `dropMessageSendCall` didn't check group-level return value

The old code dropped a dead `x = obj.foo()` assignment (promoting nothing) when the callee had no side effects — but didn't verify the group returned a consistent constant. Added `cp.getReturnValue(receiverType, methodName).isConst()` as a prerequisite, matching the same group-level check as `markDeadMethods`.

---

```java
A object;
int b;
int c;
object = new A();
b = 3;
b = object.bar(b);
c = 10;
c = c * 4;
c = c + b;
System.out.println(c);
   }
}
class A {
   public int bar(int y) {
int z;
return 6;
   }
}
class B extends A {
   public int bar(int y) {
int z;
return 9;
   }
}
```

I can see the CHA bug clearly now. `getReturnValue("A", "foo")`:

- candidates = {`A`, `B`} (B is subclass of A)

- For `B`: `declaringCls = ch.declaringClass("B","foo")` → `"B"`, **`cg.reachable("B","foo")`** → **is B.foo reachable?**

`B.foo` is only reachable if there's a call site where the receiver's declared type is `B` or a subclass of `B`. Since `object` is declared as `A`, CHA in `CallGraphVisitor` adds both `A.foo` and `B.foo` to the call graph. So `B.foo` IS reachable.

For `B.foo(x=2)`: `z = x+x = 2+2 = 4`. So both `A.foo` and `B.foo` return `CONST(4)`.
